skill.name: email-systems
skill.description: Guides email system design for transactional queues, marketing automation, deliverability, template versioning, and compliance-aware sending practices.
skill.summary: Build reliable email systems that reach inboxes and support measurable customer communication.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3 and system reconnaissance at SKILL.md:53. Both are false positives: the first is descriptive frontmatter text, and the second is a permission-based email compliance warning. No prompt injection, executable code, data exfiltration, or unsafe tool use was found in the reviewed skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line is frontmatter description text about email marketing ROI and skill scope. It does not contain cryptographic code, algorithm selection, hashing, encryption, or secret handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only descriptive prose. There is no evidence of any cryptographic operation or implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line warns against emailing people who did not opt in. This is email compliance guidance, not a command, network scan, host discovery step, or reconnaissance instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a sharp-edge table for email permission requirements. No reconnaissance behavior or executable instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Email programs often fail because queues, templates, consent, and deliverability are treated separately. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan email systems with practical safeguards.
actual_capabilities: Plans transactional email queues with retry logic and monitoring.
Defines event tracking for deliveries, opens, clicks, bounces, and complaints.
Recommends template versioning for rollback and A/B testing.
Identifies deliverability risks such as missing DNS records and IP warm-up gaps.
Highlights consent, unsubscribe, and plain-text fallback requirements.
limitations: Does not send emails or connect to email service providers.
Does not verify live DNS records or blacklist status by itself.
Does not replace legal review for marketing consent rules.
Does not include provider-specific API code or configuration files.

use_cases.title: Launch Transactional Email
use_cases.description: Design password reset, billing, and notification flows with retries, monitoring, and bounce handling.

use_cases.title: Improve Marketing Automation
use_cases.description: Plan lifecycle campaigns that use segmentation, template versions, unsubscribe handling, and clear event tracking.

use_cases.title: Audit Deliverability Risks
use_cases.description: Review email architecture for DNS setup, IP warm-up, image-heavy templates, and missing plain-text alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Email Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing sending plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this email plan for deliverability, consent, queue design, and template risks. Give prioritized fixes with short reasoning.

prompt_templates.title: Design Transactional Email
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a reliable transactional email workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a transactional email system for these events. Include queues, retries, monitoring, bounce handling, and template versioning.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Automation Events
prompt_templates.scenario: Map marketing automation and tracking needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a marketing automation plan for this product. Include segments, triggers, email events, unsubscribe handling, and testing priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Deliverability Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before scaling email volume.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this email architecture before a high-volume launch. Assess SPF, DKIM, DMARC, IP warm-up, complaints, bounces, and monitoring gaps.

output_examples.input: Review a planned password reset email flow.
output: Use a queued transactional path with retry limits and alerting.
Track delivery, bounce, and complaint events separately from marketing metrics.
Version the template so copy or link errors can be rolled back quickly.

output_examples.input: Assess a campaign made mostly of images.
output: Reduce image dependence and add meaningful text content.
Include a plain-text alternative for accessibility and spam filtering.
Confirm the unsubscribe link is visible and easy to use.

best_practices: Treat deliverability as infrastructure with monitoring, alerts, and clear ownership.
Separate transactional and marketing email paths when reliability requirements differ.
Use explicit consent records and visible unsubscribe links for marketing messages.
anti_patterns: Sending high volume from a new IP without a warm-up plan.
Using HTML-only templates without a plain-text fallback.
Ignoring bounces, complaints, or unsubscribe events after messages are sent.

faq.question: What does this skill help me design?
faq.answer: It helps design transactional email, marketing automation, deliverability checks, event tracking, and template versioning.

faq.question: Can it send emails for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning and review guidance, not live email sending or provider integration.

faq.question: Does it cover SPF, DKIM, and DMARC?
faq.answer: Yes. It flags missing sender authentication as a critical deliverability risk.

faq.question: Is this useful for non-engineers?
faq.answer: Yes. Marketers and founders can use it to plan campaigns, consent flows, and deliverability reviews.

faq.question: Does it replace legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It highlights consent and unsubscribe practices, but legal requirements need qualified review.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
